steps.title: Apply the quotient rule for exponents
steps.content:
When dividing powers with the same base, subtract the exponents. Apply this separately to $h$ and $q$:

$$\frac{h^{15}}{h^5} = h^{15-5} = h^{10}$$
$$\frac{q^{7}}{q^{21}} = q^{7-21} = q^{-14}$$

steps.title: Rewrite with positive exponents and match the choice
steps.content:
A negative exponent in the numerator moves to the denominator as a positive exponent:

$$q^{-14} = \frac{1}{q^{14}}$$

So the complete simplified expression is $\frac{h^{10}}{q^{14}}$, which matches choice A — A is correct.
